conditions.name: Bloat (Left Displaced Abomasum or Gas Bloat)
conditions.explanation: Left-sided abdominal swelling in a recently calved cow is classic for bloat, especially displaced abomasum which commonly occurs after calving

conditions.name: Post-calving digestive upset
conditions.explanation: Recent calving can cause digestive tract displacement or gas accumulation as the cow's body readjusts

conditions.name: Early stage ketosis with digestive complications
conditions.explanation: Fresh cows are at risk for metabolic disorders that can cause abdominal distension

answers.abdomen: Yes, visibly swollen on the left

records.image_observations: White chicken with healthy-looking bright red comb and wattles. Eye appears clear and alert. No visible signs of distress, discharge, or illness in this close-up head shot. Comb color contradicts pale comb typically seen in coccidiosis.
